title: equisetum fluviatile f. uliginosum
intro: Equisetum fluviatile f. uliginosum, also known as marsh horsetail, is a type of perennial plant that belongs to the Equisetaceae family, specifically a variant of the Equisetum fluviatile species. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Equisetum fluviatile f. uliginosum include reducing inflammation, improving kidney function, and promoting wound healing, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
